rule: No terminology claim may be published without a verified source entry in source_registry.json. All claims in this file remain draft or pending_source until sources are locked.

claims.statement: The English term 'bisulfide' has a lexical entry in Merriam-Webster.
claims.notes: Establishes English-language lexical existence of 'bisulfide'. Supports spelling boundary thesis.

claims.statement: The English term 'sulfide' has a lexical entry in Merriam-Webster.
claims.notes: Establishes English-language lexical existence of 'sulfide'. Supports the broader -ide/-id suffix pattern discussion.

claims.statement: The German term 'Sulfid' has a lexical entry in Duden.
claims.notes: Establishes German-language lexical existence of 'Sulfid' in an authoritative German dictionary. Supports the -id suffix form as a documented German-language standard.

claims.statement: The term 'bisulfite' refers to a distinct compound from 'bisulfide', as confirmed by a separate Merriam-Webster entry.
prohibited_uses: using bisulfite data as bisulfide data
claims.notes: Supports the core disambiguation rule: bisulfite is NOT bisulfide. Bisulfite data must never be used as bisulfide data.

claims.statement: The term 'hydrosulfides' has an entry in the IUPAC Gold Book chemical nomenclature standard.
claims.notes: Establishes IUPAC nomenclature authority for the hydrosulfide term. Supports the naming relationship between hydrosulfide and bisulfide.

claims.statement: Bisulfid appears as a German technical usage term in sulfur/water-quality monitoring context.
prohibited_uses: public proof of preferred German IUPAC spelling
claims.notes: Candidate usage claim only. Not approved for publication. Stronger source-locking required before removing source-required markers.
